text.rule: Score every PR on three axes within 60 seconds: SERVICES (1=one module, 3=one service, 5=multi-service or shared lib), USERS (1=<1%, 3=≤10%, 5=>10% of MAU), REVERSIBILITY (1=feature flag, 3=redeploy, 5=irreversible migration or external side-effect); total = sum (range 3-15).

Route by total: 3-5 → light skim (≤3 min, focus on tests + obvious smells); 6-10 → standard review (≤15 min, run locally if possible); 11-15 → deep read (≤45 min, run locally, require second pair of eyes if available).

Auto-bump to 11+ regardless of axis math when the diff touches: auth, payments, RBAC, secrets, migrations, deletion logic, money calculations, PII handling, cron schedules; these categories override the matrix.
Reversibility=5 (irreversible) PRs require a written rollback plan in the PR description, not just a rollback theoretical; missing plan blocks merge regardless of total score.
Record the score in the PR template field "Blast: S/U/R = X/Y/Z (total)" so the score is auditable post-incident; PRs without the field cannot be merged via the bot.

Recalibrate quarterly: if any score-5 PR shipped without incident for a whole quarter, re-examine whether the deep-read budget is mis-allocated; if any score-3 PR caused an incident, find what the rubric missed and add a category.
For incident triage, score blast radius the same way before paging humans: S+U+R ≥11 pages on-call now; 6-10 opens a ticket with SLA; ≤5 enters the standard bug queue.
text.example: PR #482 adds a Tailwind class to a button label — Services=1, Users=5 (visible to all), Reversibility=1 (feature flag) → total 7, but no auth/data/infra trigger → standard review, 8 minutes. PR #483 changes the password-reset email template — Services=1, Users=5, Reversibility=3 → total 9, but the auto-bump fires on auth → escalated to deep read, ran locally, caught a missing CSRF nonce, blocked merge.
